listOfQuestions.question: In Digital Media Production, what does "Resolution" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The detail of an image or video measured by pixel dimensions
wrongAnswers: The proportional relationship between width and height
A direct transition from one shot to another
Software or method used to encode and decode media

listOfQuestions.question: In Digital Media Production, what does "Frame rate" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: How many video frames are shown each second
wrongAnswers: A sequence of drawings or panels planning visual scenes
A gradual transition to or from an image or sound
The amount of data used per second of media

listOfQuestions.question: In Digital Media Production, what does "Aspect ratio" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The proportional relationship between width and height
wrongAnswers: A planned list of camera shots for production
Adjusting color and tone for style and consistency
The editing workspace where clips are arranged over time

listOfQuestions.question: In Digital Media Production, what does "Storyboard" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A sequence of drawings or panels planning visual scenes
wrongAnswers: Supplementary footage used to support the main footage
Adjusting color so whites appear neutral
A separate visual or audio element stacked with others

listOfQuestions.question: In Digital Media Production, what does "Shot list" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A planned list of camera shots for production
wrongAnswers: Narration recorded over visuals
Reducing media file size through encoding
A marker defining a property value at a point in time

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Supplementary footage used to support the main footage
wrongAnswers: A direct transition from one shot to another
